bag of cards is dealt to the author in the poker game bag of uncertainty looms large as they calculate their chances bag of hearts is their target suit and they seek to know the probability of getting at least one card of that suit in the next five cards bag of combinatorial functions is used to express this probability bag of total cards is fifty two and the author holds two so forty cards remain in the deck bag of favorable outcomes is the number of ways to get at least one heart in five cards bag of total possible outcomes is the number of ways to choose five cards from forty bag of probability is calculated as one minus the probability of getting no hearts in the five cards bag of formula is one minus combination of thirty six choose five divided by combination of forty choose five bag of simplification leads to a closed form that captures the essence of uncertainty and risk in poker

bag of choices presents itself to the author as they weigh strategy a against strategy b bag of expected value is the measure they use to decide which path to take bag of strategy a offers a 0.4 chance of winning one thousand dollars and a 0.6 chance of losing five hundred dollars bag of calculation for strategy a is 0.4 times one thousand plus 0.6 times negative five hundred bag of result is four hundred minus three hundred which equals one hundred bag of strategy b offers a 0.6 chance of winning eight hundred dollars and a 0.4 chance of losing two hundred dollars bag of calculation for strategy b is 0.6 times eight hundred plus 0.4 times negative two hundred bag of result is four hundred and eighty minus eighty which equals four hundred bag of expected value for strategy b is higher than that of strategy a bag of decision is clear the author should choose strategy b to maximize their expected value

bag of words is a palindrome in itself and echoes the symmetry of choices and outcomes in the game of life and poker
